_provenance.stage: Stage 1 — derived from live cards for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: Four Undangs Declared the Ruler Deposed. Three Hold Office. One Does Not.
context: On 19 April 2026, the four Undangs of Negeri Sembilan declared Tuanku Muhriz deposed and named Tunku Nadzaruddin the proposed 12th Ruler. State government rejects it: one Undang was dismissed in May 2025.

cards.text: Four Undangs declared Tuanku Muhriz deposed and named Tunku Nadzaruddin the 12th Ruler. Three hold office; one was dismissed 11 months earlier.
cards.sub: State government rejects the declaration. The same Undang who proclaimed Muhriz in 2008 (since removed for 33 offences) signed this one too.

cards.h: The constitution lets the Undangs collectively call the Ruler to withdraw. Whether three alone (without the dismissed fourth) suffice is untested.
cards.s: BERNAMA and Malay Mail cite Articles 10-12; The Star cites 10-13. All agree: rejection rests on Mubarak no longer holding office.
cards.d: The constitution lets the Undangs collectively call the Ruler to withdraw. Whether three alone (without the dismissed fourth) suffice is untested. BERNAMA and Malay Mail cite Articles 10-12; The Star cites 10-13. All agree: rejection rests on Mubarak no longer holding office.

cards.h: The same Undang who read the deposal on 19 April 2026 had proclaimed Tuanku Muhriz as 11th Ruler on 29 December 2008.
cards.s: In 1967, the Undang bypassed Muhriz for the throne at 19, electing his uncle Tuanku Ja'afar. Tunku Nadzaruddin (Ja'afar's son) is now the proposed successor.
cards.d: The same Undang who read the deposal on 19 April 2026 had proclaimed Tuanku Muhriz as 11th Ruler on 29 December 2008. In 1967, the Undang bypassed Muhriz for the throne at 19, electing his uncle Tuanku Ja'afar. Tunku Nadzaruddin (Ja'afar's son) is now the proposed successor.

cards.h: Mubarak was dismissed 13 May 2025 for 33 offences against Telaga Undang custom. The DKU ratified the dismissal two days before he signed the declaration.
cards.s: The DKU sat 17 April 2026, presided by Tuanku Muhriz. Ibu Soko Klana Hulu and Anak Waris Klana Hulu declined to give Mubarak a mandate.
cards.d: Mubarak was dismissed 13 May 2025 for 33 offences against Telaga Undang custom. The DKU ratified the dismissal two days before he signed the declaration. The DKU sat 17 April 2026, presided by Tuanku Muhriz. Ibu Soko Klana Hulu and Anak Waris Klana Hulu declined to give Mubarak a mandate.

cards.h: Undangs cited alleged royal misconduct from a 5 March inquiry. Institutions called the act menderhaka; JAIS and police were notified.
cards.s: No state, customary, or judicial body has publicly addressed the misconduct claim itself. Daulat was defended before the underlying allegation was examined.
cards.d: Undangs cited alleged royal misconduct from a 5 March inquiry. Institutions called the act menderhaka; JAIS and police were notified. No state, customary, or judicial body has publicly addressed the misconduct claim itself. Daulat was defended before the underlying allegation was examined.

cards.text: The real question is not whether one dismissed Undang can act. It is whether three Undangs alone can invoke a provision designed for four.
A constitution built on the collective authority of four Undangs is being tested by three. No court has ruled, and the institutions have defended the throne first.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
